pattern: Any integer that divides both numbers must divide their greatest common divisor, so the answer is simply the count of divisors of that gcd.
hints: A common factor divides both a and b at the same time.
Every common divisor of two numbers is also a divisor of their greatest common divisor.
Compute gcd(a, b) and then count how many integers from one up to it divide it evenly.

edge_inputs.description: equal values share all divisors of the value
one is one so only common factor is one

stress.description: random pairs across the range to exercise varied gcds
stress.note: covers coprime, divisor, and composite gcd cases
